This submodule change cannot be added to: This submodule change cannot be added to a commit in this repository because it contains changes that have not been committed.
This submodule has modified and untracke: This submodule has modified and untracked changes. Those changes must be committed inside of the submodule before they can be part of the parent repository.

This submodule has untracked changes. Th: This submodule has untracked changes. Those changes must be committed inside of the submodule before they can be part of the parent repository.

Undoing a merge commit will apply the ch: Undoing a merge commit will apply the changes from the merge into your working directory, and committing again will create an entirely new commit. This means you will lose the merge commit and, as a result, commits from the merged branch could disappear from this branch.

When enabled, check marks will be displa: When enabled, check marks will be displayed along side the line numbers and groups of line numbers in the diff when committing. When disabled, the line number controls will be less prominent.
When enabled, GitHub Desktop will attemp: When enabled, GitHub Desktop will attempt to load environment variables from your shell when executing Git hooks. This is useful if your Git hooks depend on environment variables set in your shell configuration files, a common practice for version managers such as nvm, rbenv, asdf, etc.
